title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. insigne
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. insigne, also known as Yellow Buttons, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to southeastern Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Yellow Buttons include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with colds, fever, and skin issues, as well as its ability to support wound healing and immune function. Herbal preparations of Yellow Buttons typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
